name: Dumbbell Wrist Curl
resistance_profile: Heaviest at the bottom, where the dumbbell rolls to the fingertips and the wrist flexors are fully stretched; easier as the wrist curls up.
cues: Forearm on your thigh, palm up
Let the dumbbell roll to the fingertips
Curl up through a full range
execution_steps: Sit and rest one forearm along your thigh (or a bench), palm facing up, with your wrist just past your knee.
Hold a dumbbell and let it roll down to your fingertips for a full stretch at the bottom.
Curl the dumbbell up by flexing only your wrist as high as it will go.
Lower under control back to the fingertip stretch, then repeat; switch arms.
good_when: You want to train each forearm one at a time to fix left-right imbalances.
You want a deep, loaded stretch on the wrist flexors.
bad_when: You want to load both wrists heavily at once, where a barbell wrist curl is more time-efficient.
You are targeting the wrist extensors, which need a reverse wrist curl.
You want a longer set without re-racking between arms.
